narrationOverrides.problem: Buying a SKU is not the same as knowing whether the user has the right to a feature right now. Renewals, grace periods, refunds, billing retry, product changes, trials, family sharing and store server notifications all show up later. The device has one view. The store has another. Your backend usually needs a third.

selected.focus: Separating portable integrity signals from a respectful store-review flow
selected.query: mobile app attestation RASP app review API

narrationOverrides.tradeoffs: The app cannot certify itself. An attestation token is opaque, and the block-or-allow decision belongs on your backend. Runtime self-protection raises the cost of an attack; it does not prove a device is clean.
narrationOverrides.result: AppReview applies the same restraint to engagement. State lives in Preferences, and once a user rates or opts out, they are never prompted again. The native prompt or fallback is only useful when the timing respects the user.
narrationOverrides.short-boundary: The app cannot certify itself. An attestation token is opaque, and the block-or-allow decision belongs on your backend. Runtime self-protection raises the cost of an attack; it does not prove a device is clean.

selected.focus: A sustainable open-source business boundary that does not sabotage the free product
selected.query: sustainable open source business model

narrationOverrides.hook: Open source projects often end in one of two failures: the maintainers run out of money and the project rots, or the company waits for lock-in and quietly takes the freedoms back. Codename One has spent fourteen years building a third path.
narrationOverrides.problem: Codename One the open source project and Codename One the company are not the same thing. The hard part is funding the company without making the open project worse so the paid product looks better.
narrationOverrides.mechanism: The project stays under the GNU GPL with the Classpath Exception. The exception keeps your applications yours; the copyleft keeps Codename One free and forkable. The company charges for metered build-cloud capacity and for optional services layered on top.
narrationOverrides.source: Analytics shows the boundary in code. The open AnalyticsProvider SPI can target GA4, Matomo, Firebase, your own backend, or Codename One's paid provider. Choosing the first-party service funds the project; refusing it does not cripple the API.
narrationOverrides.tradeoffs: This model only works if the paid service wins through integration depth instead of lock-in. Crash symbolication can be seamless because the company runs the build servers, while the open project and alternative providers remain usable without it.
narrationOverrides.result: The promise is concrete: no relicensing trap, no sabotaged free tier, and no tax on the freedom you already have. Optional services must earn adoption by being the better default, while financing the next port, API, and year of maintenance.
narrationOverrides.short-hook: Open source projects often rot when maintainers run out of money, or turn source-available after the community is locked in. Codename One has spent fourteen years building a third path.
narrationOverrides.short-problem: The problem is funding the company without making the open project worse so the paid product looks better.
narrationOverrides.short-mechanism: The project stays GPL with the Classpath Exception, so your apps remain yours and Codename One remains free and forkable. Revenue comes from metered build-cloud capacity and optional services layered on top.
narrationOverrides.short-boundary: Analytics shows the rule: one open provider SPI can target GA4, Matomo, Firebase, your backend, or Codename One's paid provider. The paid option must win through integration, not lock-in.

selected.focus: Building a playable Java platformer from sprite data, collision shapes, and rules
selected.query: Java 2D platformer tutorial
sectionOrder: Why a game builder?
What just got saved? The .game file
Loading and showing the game
Preview, runtime, and the arcade behavior
Your rules — winning, power-ups, and death

narrationOverrides.hook: A platformer level should not be hundreds of coordinates hidden in Java. In Codename One's Game Builder, the scene is readable, diffable data; Java is reserved for the rules that make your game yours.
narrationOverrides.problem: Hand-placing every sprite in code makes layout invisible until runtime and forces a rebuild for each tweak. Designers cannot safely move a platform, while developers waste time editing coordinates instead of behavior.
narrationOverrides.mechanism: The editor saves layers, tiles, actors, transforms, and typed properties in a plain game file. The runtime realizes that data into a Scene, while the generated companion class binds named objects such as player, enemy, and flag to Java fields.
narrationOverrides.source: The companion class loads CoffeeRun.game, calls initScene, and enables the arcade behavior. That one boundary turns the visual level into a GameView while keeping the constructor and update loop available for your own logic.
narrationOverrides.tradeoffs: The editor preview and the shipped GameSceneView are different runtimes. Calling setArcadeBehavior true is what makes gravity, running, jumping, collision, patrols, pickups, and the follow camera match. You can override each protected hook, or disable the defaults entirely.
narrationOverrides.result: Now level iteration needs no rebuild: move coffee, change gravity, or repaint terrain in the editor. Java only adds rules the engine cannot infer, such as winning when Duke overlaps the flag, custom power-ups, checkpoints, or a death screen.
narrationOverrides.short-hook: A platformer level should not be hundreds of coordinates hidden in Java. Store the scene as readable data and keep Java for the rules.
narrationOverrides.short-problem: Hand-placing sprites in code makes layout invisible until runtime and forces a rebuild for every tweak.
narrationOverrides.short-mechanism: The Game Builder saves layers, tiles, actors, transforms, and typed properties in a plain game file. A generated companion binds named objects to Java fields.
narrationOverrides.short-boundary: Preview and runtime are different. setArcadeBehavior true enables matching movement, collision, patrols, pickups, and camera behavior; every hook remains overridable.

narrationOverrides.source: The runtime loads the plain game file, realizes its layers and elements into a Scene, wraps that Scene in a GameView, and starts the loop. The editor owns level data; this small Java boundary owns application behavior.

narrationOverrides.source: Scrubbing happens on the device before upload. You can extend PiiScrubber, run the default email and digit rules first, then replace application-specific identifiers such as account numbers before CrashProtection is installed.
